layout.searchPatients: Search patients by name or ID

home.headline: Compassionate Care,
home.subtext: UmitAI bridges the gap between clinic visits with daily symptom tracking, verified psychological assessments, and direct communication with your healthcare team.
home.continuousTrackingDescription: Log your daily symptoms like pain and fatigue to help your doctors understand your wellness journey.
home.psychologicalSupportDescription: Take clinical HADS tests and receive immediate recommendations to support your mental health.
home.secureCommunicationDescription: Chat securely with your assigned psychologist and medical team when you need them most.
home.demoDescription: Choose a role to view the dashboard without changing your real login state.

auth.enterDetails: Enter your details to access your portal
auth.registerStart: Register to start your supported journey
auth.forEmergencies: For medical emergencies, please call emergency services immediately.

patientDashboard.overview: Here is the overview of your wellness program for today.
patientDashboard.symptomDiaryDescription: You haven't logged your symptoms today. Tracking helps us help you better.
patientDashboard.hadsAssessmentDescription: Your weekly psychological assessment is due. It takes ~5 minutes.
patientDashboard.messagesDescription: Dr. Emily Smith (Psychologist) sent you a new message 2 hours ago.
patientDashboard.newRecommendationsDescription: Based on your recent tests, we've updated your daily wellness guide.
patientDashboard.sleepStableDescription: Your sleep tracking shows positive improvement over the last 3 days.

patientProfile.title: Patient Profile
patientProfile.subtitle: Manage your personal data, current diagnosis, and treatment plan details.

testInterface.title: HADS Assessment
testInterface.subtitle: Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale
testInterface.instruction: Please select the reply that is closest to how you have been feeling in the past week. Don't take too long over your replies.
testInterface.question2: I still enjoy the things I used to enjoy:
testInterface.question3: I get a sort of frightened feeling as if something awful is about to happen:
testInterface.question4: I can laugh and see the funny side of things:
testInterface.option3c: A little, but it doesn't worry me (1)
testInterface.option4a: As much as I always could (0)

testResults.title: Assessment Results
testResults.subtitle: Your latest HADS scores and historical progress.
testResults.anxietyDescription: Your anxiety levels are currently within the normal clinical range. A decrease of 1 point from your last assessment shows positive progress.
testResults.depressionDescription: Your depression score indicates no clinical concern at this time. Maintain your current routines and support structures.

symptomDiary.title: Symptom Diary
symptomDiary.subtitle: Track your daily wellness to help us adjust your care plan.
symptomDiary.alertText: Your reported levels are high. Our system has notified your care team, and they will reach out shortly.

chat.title: Care Team Chat
chat.subtitle: Direct communication with your assigned psychologist and medical team.
chat.makeSure: Make sure you are taking adequ...
chat.message1: Hello Sarah. I saw your recent HADS test results. It looks like you've been doing well with anxiety management.
chat.message2: Yes Dr. Smith, the breathing exercises you recommended really helped, especially before going to sleep.
chat.message3: That is excellent news! I noticed your fatigue scores are slightly up though. Make sure you are taking adequate rest periods during the day.

recommendations.title: AI Supported Recommendations
recommendations.description: Based on your recent HADS results and Symptom Diary stating elevated fatigue, we have synthesized the following supportive actions tailored specifically to your active treatment phase.
recommendations.sleepNote: Your fatigue score has averaged 6/10 this week.
recommendations.activeResting: Implement 'active resting' - taking structured 20-minute rests before you feel completely exhausted.
recommendations.screenTime: Limit screen time 1 hour before scheduled sleep to improve sleep quality.
recommendations.medicationTiming: If you experience pain at night, consult Dr. Smith about adjusting medication timing.
recommendations.movementNote: Gentle movement maintains muscle mass without depleting energy.
recommendations.yogaSequence: Try the recommended 15-minute gentle seated yoga sequence.
recommendations.shortWalks: Aim for two 10-minute walks rather than one 20-minute walk to conserve energy reserves.
recommendations.nutritionNote: While your appetite score is stable, maintaining nutritional intake during phase 2 treatment is critical to combating fatigue.
recommendations.hydrationText: Aim for 2.5L daily. SIP slowly throughout the day rather than drinking large amounts at once to prevent mild nausea.
recommendations.proteinText: Include small amounts of high-biological value protein (Greek yogurt, eggs) in every snack to stabilize energy.
recommendations.gingerText: Recommended to introduce ginger tea post-meals to pre-emptively manage trace nausea.

doctorDashboard.title: Psychologist/Doctor Dashboard
doctorDashboard.subtitle: Overview of your patient roster and critical alerts requiring attention.

patientAnalytics.title: Patient Analytics View
patientAnalytics.patientDetails: 38 Y/O • Female • Breast Cancer Stage II • Active Treatment
patientAnalytics.systemAlertText: Patient has logged consecutive days of fatigue > 7/10. AI recommendation logic triggered protocol 2B (Rest management).
patientAnalytics.combinedDescription: Visualizing HADS scores against reported Physical Symptoms to identify psychosocial impact factors.
patientAnalytics.aiInsightText: Historical data indicates a strong positive correlation between spikes in physical Pain and subsequent increases in HADS Anxiety scores (lag time ~2 days). Current fatigue spike is isolated; monitor pain diligently to prevent psych regression.
